title: heracleum sphondylium var. akasimontanum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium var. akasimontanum, also known as wild celery or cow parsley, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Asia. It is a member of the carrot family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's leaves and stems contain a high concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid with antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage and promote overall health benefits such as improved digestion and reduced inflammation. Wild celery can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and its extracts have been used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, fever, and digestive problems.
